title: jasione amethystina subsp. mariana
intro: Jasione amethystina subsp. mariana, also known as the subalpine bellflower, is a plant species that belongs to the bellflower family and is native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The subalpine bellflower can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties. However, further research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential risks associated with using this plant for medicinal purposes.
